L1.area: Scripting language runtime and expression interpretation

L2.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists where in-place mutation optimizations for data merging and patching operations use unsafe memory transmutation to convert immutable references to mutable ones. When a variable is both the target of such an operation and the assignment destination, the operation can insert references to data from another source. After that source data is freed, those references become dangling, allowing access to freed or reallocated memory through stale string keys or values.

L3.area: Scripting language runtime and expression interpretation
L3.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists where in-place mutation optimizations for data merging and patching operations use unsafe memory transmutation to convert immutable references to mutable ones. When a variable is both the target of such an operation and the assignment destination, the operation can insert references to data from another source. After that source data is freed, those references become dangling, allowing access to freed or reallocated memory through stale string keys or values.

ground_truth.reason: The patch_in_place and merge_in_place optimization functions use unsafe transmute to convert immutable references to mutable ones, enabling in-place mutation that can insert references to event data into state. After the event is freed, those references become dangling pointers.
